title: Automated DNA Extraction from Sediment Samples and Filter Membranes Using Magnetic Beads
author: Qianyue Ji

steps.title: Use 1 g of each sediment...
steps.instruction: Use 1 g of each sediment sample or one half of each filter membrane.

steps.title: Sediment samples were centrifuged in 96-well...
steps.instruction: Sediment samples were centrifuged in 96-well deep-well plates (3960, Corning) at 12.000 rpm, °C, for 00:05:00 before the extraction, and the supernatant was discarded before extraction.

steps.title: Filter membranes were cut to small...
steps.instruction: Filter membranes were cut to small pieces and subjected to extraction directly.

steps.title: For one reaction, every 20 µL...
steps.instruction: For one reaction, every 20 µL Magnetic Beads-T are mixed with 300 µL PFB-M, and mix them thoroughly.

steps.title: Take out 5 U-bottom deep-well plates...
steps.instruction: Take out 5 U-bottom deep-well plates (MGI, 1000004644), and mark them as 【PFBM + Magnetic beads-T】, 【PW1-M】, 【PW2】, and 【PB】respectively. Add reagents according to Table. Item Volume/Well 1 PFBM + Magnetic beads-T 320 μL (PFB-M + Magnetic beads-T mixed liquor) + 240 μL sample 2 PW1-M 400 μL 3 PW2 800 μL 4 PB 120 μL Input volume of PFB-M, PW1-M, PW2, Magnetic Beads-T, and PB

steps.title: Select script provided by the supplier for DNA extraction
steps.instruction: Select script provided by the supplier for DNA extraction.

steps.title: The extracted DNA can be stored at -20 °C
steps.instruction: The extracted DNA can be stored at -20 °C .

steps.title: Follow the on-screen instructions to place...
steps.instruction: Follow the on-screen instructions to place the consumables, samples, and reagents. Confirm the placement and close the door.
